originalTitle: Witnessing joy amid the death: BBC travels to epicentre of Ebola outbreak
title: Big Smiles and Helping Hands: Our Friends in Congo Are Strong!
content:
A Big Hello from Africa!

Hello, my amazing friends all across Africa! Your special news reporter is here with an important story. Sometimes, people can get a little bit sick. And right now, in a beautiful country in Africa called the Democratic Republic of Congo, some of our friends are working very, very hard to help people feel better from a special sickness. It’s like a tiny, tiny bug that can make you feel unwell. But guess what? There’s a lot of happiness and bravery too! People are getting better, and everyone is helping each other with big, kind hearts.

What is This Tiny Bug, Ebola?

Let's talk about this tiny bug called Ebola. It’s a very small germ, so tiny you can’t even see it with your eyes! It can make people feel very tired, like they want to sleep all day. It can give them a fever, which means their body feels too hot. Sometimes, it can make their tummy hurt too. It’s not like a normal cold that goes away quickly. This tiny bug needs special helpers to make it go away. Did you know? Viruses are super, super small, even smaller than a tiny speck of dust you might see in the sunshine! That's why we can't see them coming! It’s important to remember that this tiny bug is not everywhere in Africa. It is in some special places in Congo. But don't worry, many clever doctors and nurses know just what to do to help people fight it off. They are like real-life superheroes!

Where Are Our Friends in Congo?

The Democratic Republic of Congo, or DRC for short, is a very big and beautiful country in the middle of Africa. It has big, green forests, mighty rivers, and lots of wonderful animals. Our African family lives there. The sickness called Ebola is happening in some parts of this big country, not all of it. Imagine your own country; the sickness is like a small spot in a very big place. Did you know? The Democratic Republic of Congo is home to amazing animals like gentle gorillas and okapis, which look a bit like zebras but with long necks! It's a land full of wonders. Even though the tiny bug is making some people sick, the people of Congo are very strong. They are working together every single day to make things better. Their spirit is bright and full of hope, just like the African sun.

Superheroes in Action: Fighting the Sickness!

In Congo, there are so many superheroes! These are the doctors, nurses, and other helpers. They wear very special clothes, like big suits and masks, to keep themselves safe while they help others. It's a bit like an astronaut's suit! They check on people who are sick, give them special drinks and medicine, and make sure they are comfortable. Did you know? Washing your hands with soap and water for 20 whole seconds can wash away many tiny bugs! That's like singing the 'Happy Birthday' song twice, very slowly! It's a super important thing to do to stay healthy. These heroes are so brave. They work day and night because they care so much about everyone getting well. They show us all how important it is to help our neighbours and friends when they need us most. They are true examples for all of us.

Good News! Happy Smiles Are Back!

Now for the best news! Even though some people get sick, many, many people get better! Their bodies fight off the tiny bug, and they become strong again. When someone gets well, it’s a time for big celebrations! People sing songs, dance with joy, and clap their hands. They are so happy to go back home to their families. Did you know? When someone gets better from Ebola, their body becomes very strong against that tiny bug, so it's harder for them to get sick from it again! Their body learns how to be a super fighter! These happy moments show us that even when things are tough, there is always hope and a reason to smile. These smiles are like sunshine, telling everyone that we can beat this sickness together. It reminds us of the strength of the African spirit, which is full of courage and happiness.

Everyone is a Helper!

It's not just the doctors and nurses who are helping. Everyone in Congo is a helper! Mothers and fathers teach their children about washing hands and staying clean. Grandparents share wise words and comfort. Community leaders talk to everyone about how to stay safe and healthy. They make sure everyone knows what to do to protect themselves and their families. Did you know? When we all work together, like a big team playing soccer or building a sandcastle, we are much, much stronger! We can solve big problems when we help each other. They share food and kind words. They show us that being a good neighbour and friend is very important. When we help each other, we make our whole community, and even our whole continent of Africa, a safer and happier place to live. Every small act of kindness makes a big difference!

How Can You Be a Smart Kid?

So, what can you do, my smart little friends? You can be a super helper too! First, don’t be scared, but be smart. Always remember to wash your hands often with soap and water, especially before you eat and after you play. Listen to your parents and teachers because they know how to keep you safe. If you ever feel unwell, always tell a grown-up right away. Did you know? Eating yummy fruits and vegetables every day helps your body stay super strong and gives you energy to play and learn! A strong body can fight off tiny bugs better. Stay clean, eat good food, and play outside in the fresh air. These are all ways you can help your body stay strong and healthy. And remember to always be kind to your friends and family. A happy heart also helps you stay strong!

Let's Keep Our African Spirit Strong!

My dear friends, the people in the Democratic Republic of Congo are brave and strong. They are working so hard, and they are finding joy even when things are difficult. They show us that with love, kindness, and teamwork, we can overcome challenges. We send big hugs and happy thoughts to our friends in Congo. Let’s keep our African spirit strong, full of courage and hope. We are all one big family, and we care for each other. We will keep learning and growing, and together, we will make sure everyone in Africa can live a healthy and happy life. Keep smiling, my amazing friends!
summary: Brave people in the Democratic Republic of Congo are working hard to fight a sickness called Ebola, and many are getting well, celebrating with big smiles and helping each other.
whyMatters: This story matters because it shows us how strong and brave people are when they work together to help each other get healthy and safe, even when facing a tough sickness.

originalTitle: South African jazz legend Abdullah Ibrahim dies at 91
title: Remembering Abdullah Ibrahim: A South African Music Hero
content:
Hello, Music Lovers!

Hello, everyone! We have some news about a very, very special person from South Africa. His name was Abdullah Ibrahim. He was a wonderful musician who played the piano like magic. Sadly, he has gone to rest. He was 91 years old. That's a very long life, full of beautiful music!

Who Was Abdullah Ibrahim?

Abdullah Ibrahim was born in a beautiful country called South Africa. He loved music very much, even when he was a little boy. He grew up to be one of the most famous musicians from our amazing continent, Africa. He played the piano, and his music made many people feel happy, hopeful, and sometimes even like dancing! **Did you know?** A piano has many keys, and when you press them, tiny hammers inside hit strings to make beautiful sounds! It's like a big, clever musical machine.

A Piano Wizard

Imagine someone who can tell the most amazing stories, but instead of using words, they use music. That was Abdullah Ibrahim! He made the piano sing with special sounds. He was so, so good that people called him a "legend." A legend is someone who is super famous and super good at what they do. Like a superhero of music!

What is Jazz Music?

Abdullah Ibrahim played a special kind of music called "jazz." Jazz music is often very lively and free. Musicians often make up parts of the music as they play, right there on the spot! This is called improvising. It's like having a fun, musical conversation with your instrument! Jazz often has a rhythm that makes you want to tap your feet or sway. **Did you know?** Jazz music started a long, long time ago in America, but then it travelled all over the world and mixed with music from different countries and cultures!

South African Jazz: A Special Sound

Abdullah Ibrahim didn't just play jazz; he made it his very own. He mixed the lively sounds of jazz with the amazing traditional music of South Africa. It was like taking a delicious recipe and adding a secret, special ingredient that only he knew how to use! This made his music sound unique and full of the beautiful spirit of South Africa.

Music from His Home

His music often made people think of the beautiful landscapes and the strong, hopeful spirit of South Africa. When you listened to his songs, you could almost hear the wind blowing across the vast plains or feel the warmth of the African sun. His music told stories without using any words at all. It was truly magical and heartfelt.

Playing Music for a Very, Very Long Time

Abdullah Ibrahim played music for a super long time. The news says he played for "eight decades." Do you know how long a decade is? One decade is ten years! So, eight decades means he played music for eighty years! Can you imagine doing something you love so much for eighty years? That's almost his whole, wonderful life! **Did you know?** Many famous musicians start learning their instruments when they are very young, sometimes even before they go to big school, just like you might be learning to read or draw now!

Travelling the World with Music

Because his music was so special and unique, Abdullah Ibrahim travelled all around the world. He played his piano in many different countries and for many different people. People everywhere loved to hear his special South African jazz. He shared a beautiful piece of South Africa with everyone he met through his amazing melodies.

Making People Feel Hopeful

During a difficult time in South Africa's history, when people were not treated fairly because of their skin colour, Abdullah Ibrahim's music was like a comforting hug. His songs gave people hope and strength. They reminded everyone of the beauty and joy that lived in the hearts of South Africans, even when things were tough.

A Message of Peace

His music carried a strong and beautiful message of peace and coming together. It showed that even when things are challenging, music can bring people closer. It helped people dream of a brighter future where everyone was kind to each other and lived in harmony. That's a very powerful and wonderful thing for music to do! **Did you know?** Music can make you feel many different emotions – happy, sad, calm, or even super excited! It's like a special language for our feelings.

Remembering a Giant

Now, Abdullah Ibrahim has left us, but his music will never go away. It will live on forever, playing in our homes and our hearts. He showed us that with passion and hard work, you can share your special gifts with the whole world. He was a true giant of music, a big, bright star.

His Musical Gifts Live On

Even though he is not here with us anymore, we can still listen to his amazing songs. We can remember his kind, smiling face and the way his fingers danced so gracefully on the piano keys. Every time we hear his music, it's like he is still playing for us, sharing his joy.

Let's Listen to His Tunes!

So, next time you hear some beautiful piano music, especially jazz, think of Abdullah Ibrahim. Maybe you can even ask your parents or teachers to play some of his songs for you. You might find a new favourite tune that makes your heart happy! **Did you know?** Many famous songs are played on the radio or online for many, many years, even after the person who made them is gone. That's how their music stays alive and keeps making new friends!

A Legacy of Love and Sound

Abdullah Ibrahim left behind a wonderful "legacy." A legacy means all the good things he did and shared that will be remembered long after he's gone. His legacy is his beautiful music and the joy and hope it brought to so many people around the world. We celebrate his life and all the amazing music he gave us. Thank you, Abdullah Ibrahim, for sharing your magic with the world! You will be missed, but your music will live forever.
summary: Abdullah Ibrahim, a kind and famous piano player from South Africa, who made beautiful jazz music for 80 years, has gone to rest at 91 years old, leaving behind a legacy of joyful and hopeful songs for everyone to remember.
whyMatters: Abdullah Ibrahim's music was very special because it mixed jazz with South African sounds, making people feel happy and hopeful, especially during hard times, and his music will keep living on to inspire us all.
